label: Hot Reload / Testing
title: Debugging, testing, and hot reload
lead: Are you tired of restarting the game to test your mod?
bodyHtml:
Fast mod-testing loop

Most CUCoreLib iteration gets faster when you separate startup-only work from content registration. Keep console commands, Harmony patches, and other one-time setup in Awake(), but move reloadable content into a dedicated host class or explicit replay methods.

That split helps even before hot reload enters the picture. However, hot reload makes this much much faster. Let's learn how to use it.

Usage

The preferred entrypoint contract is a class-level opt-in on a content host:

[CCLContentHost]
internal sealed class FantasyContent : CCLBase
{
    public void CacheMyAssets() { ... }
    public void AddMyItems() { ... }

    [ContentReloadEntry(ContentReloadEntryStage.RegisterRecipes, Order = 10)]
    public void AddLateRecipes() { ... }

    [CCLReloadIgnore]
    public void DebugSpawnItem() { ... }
}

Inside a [CCLContentHost] class, CUCoreLib scans parameterless methods, infers the reload stage from the registry calls it finds, and replays supported methods in stage order. Use [ContentReloadEntry(...)] only when you need an explicit stage or custom ordering, and [CCLReloadIgnore] to opt a helper out.

Static host classes work too, which means existing FantasyContent-style files can opt in with a single class attribute. If you prefer an instance-backed host, derive from CCLBase and call CCLBase.Initialize(this) from your plugin Awake().

Commands


      reloadcontent com.example.mymod
      autohotreload C:/Users/you/source/MyMod/bin/Debug/net48/MyMod.dll true
      autohotreload C:/Users/you/source/MyMod/bin/Debug/net48/MyMod.dll false
  • reloadcontent <modGuid> loads the rebuilt DLL, clears the previous content, and replays only recognized content methods.
  • autohotreload <filepath> true enables persistent watch mode for that DLL, which automatically hot reloads if it detects a file hash change.
  • autohotreload <filepath> false disables the saved watch mode for that DLL.

Automatic discovery rules

  • Supported v1 surfaces are locale/text, liquids, items, basic building entities, and recipes.
  • Method names do not matter. CUCoreLib classifies host methods by what they register.
  • Asset loading can stay inline with registration, or live in a host method that only loads assets.
  • If a method mixes multiple supported registration surfaces, CUCoreLib skips it and tells you to split it.
  • If a method mixes registration with unsupported side effects such as scene spawning, tiles, structures, save hooks, or Harmony setup, CUCoreLib skips it with a diagnostic instead of replaying it.

Limitations

CUCoreLib's built-in DLL reload path is intentionally narrow. It is singleplayer only and only reloads locale/text, liquid, item, basic building, and recipe content. It does not rerun Awake(), and it does not support tiles, structures, save providers, moodles, Harmony patches, mod options, console command registration, or multiplayer registration just yet.

When strict reload skips a discovered method, the reload summary reports the exact method and why it was skipped.

Testing in multiplayer

  • In Casualties Unknown Demo\CasualtiesUnknown_Data\boot.config, remove the forceSingleInstance: 1 line.
  • Open the multiplayer mod on two instances and swap from Steam to IP hosting.
  • The name of the client and the host must be different.
  • Connect on both, and you can test multiplayer interactions.

Launch overrides and optimizations

For testing elements that are not covered by hot reload, CUCoreLib has a configuration for loading directly into a test world or tutorial.

In BepInEx -> Config -> CUCoreLib.cfg, there are options to instantly load the debug world or tutorial on launch.

Similarly, you may want to consider a batch script for automating the testing process. For instance, a .bat script:


@echo off
setlocal EnableExtensions

set "GAME_DIR=C:Program Files (x86)SteamsteamappscommonCasualties Unknown Demo"
set "PLUGIN_DIR=%GAME_DIR%BepInExplugins"
set "SOURCE_DLL=PATH_TO_binDebugFILENAME.dll"

echo Closing scav
taskkill /F /IM "CasualtiesUnknown.exe" >nul 2>&1
timeout /t 1 /nobreak >nul

echo.
echo Copying .dll file
copy /Y "%SOURCE_DLL%" "%PLUGIN_DIR%" >nul

echo.
echo Launching through Steam...
start "" "steam://rungameid/4576510"

timeout /t 3 /nobreak >nul
tasklist /FI "IMAGENAME eq CasualtiesUnknown.exe" | find /I "CasualtiesUnknown.exe" >nul
if errorlevel 1 (
    echo Steam launch did not start the game (offline?). Falling back to local executable...
    start "" "%GAME_DIR%CasualtiesUnknown.exe"
)
endlocal
exit /b 0
